verdict_reasoning: TYK2 is a soluble cytoplasmic Janus kinase that associates with the cytoplasmic (intracellular) domains of type I and II cytokine receptors. It has no transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, or other outer-leaflet anchoring mechanism. It functions as an intracellular signaling enzyme recruited transiently to the inner face of the membrane via cytokine receptor binding, not as a surface-exposed protein accessible from outside the cell.
